request.prompt: We're burning $50k/month and have 6 months of runway. What should I prioritize?

response.message:
Mission received. Prioritize immediate revenue generation and runway extension. Here's the tactical breakdown:

1. **Revenue First**: Identify and deploy high-impact monetization levers within 30 days.
2. **Cost Control**: Freeze non-essential spend; renegotiate vendor contracts.
3. **Runway Extension**: Secure bridge funding or convertible note to add 3+ months.
4. **Core Metric Focus**: Double down on the single metric that drives customer value and retention.

Execute in that order. Status: Advising.

request.prompt: I need to launch a Black Friday campaign across email, social media, and Google Ads. Budget is $10k. Make me a workflow that covers everything from creative to post-campaign analysis.

response.message: Mission received. Building Black Friday campaign launch workflow with full lifecycle coverage.

nodes.label: Campaign Brief & Budget
nodes.description: Initial requirements: $10k budget, multi-channel (email, social, Google Ads), Black Friday focus.
nodes.content: Objective: Launch Black Friday campaign to drive sales across channels. Budget: $10,000 allocated. Channels: Email marketing, Social Media (Meta, TikTok), Google Ads (Search, Shopping). Timeline: Pre-launch (2 weeks), Campaign week (Black Friday week), Post-campaign (1 week analysis). KPIs: ROAS, conversion rate, email open rate, social engagement, ad CTR.

nodes.label: Creative & Asset Development
nodes.description: Design and produce campaign creatives: email templates, social posts, ad copy, banners, video snippets.
nodes.content: Tasks: 1. Design Black Friday email template (mobile-responsive). 2. Create 10 social media creatives (static/graphics, 3 short videos). 3. Write Google Ads copy (5 headlines, 3 descriptions per ad group). 4. Produce banner ads (300x250, 728x90). Assets due 2 weeks pre-launch. Tools: Figma, Canva, Adobe Suite. Approval required before next step.

nodes.label: Channel Setup & Segmentation
nodes.description: Configure email lists, social ad accounts, Google Ads campaigns, audience targeting.
nodes.content: Email: Segment list (past buyers, subscribers, inactive). Build flows (welcome series, cart abandonment). Social: Set up Meta Ads Manager, TikTok Ads. Create audiences (lookalikes, retargeting). Google Ads: Create Search campaigns (keywords: 'black friday deals'), Shopping feed upload. Budget allocation: Email $2k, Social $4k, Google Ads $4k. Launch scheduled for Black Friday week.

nodes.label: Pre-Launch QA & Compliance
nodes.description: Test all assets, links, tracking, and ensure compliance (CAN-SPAM, ad policies).
nodes.content: QA checklist: 1. All links functional (UTM tracking applied). 2. Email spam score check. 3. Ad approvals (Meta, Google). 4. Landing page load speed <3s. 5. Pixel tracking verified (Facebook Pixel, Google Analytics). Compliance: Include unsubscribe in emails, ad disclosures (#ad). Sign-off required from legal/marketing lead.

nodes.label: Campaign Launch Trigger
nodes.description: Manual go-live signal to activate all channels simultaneously.
nodes.content: Launch command issued. All channels go live: Email broadcast sent to segments. Social ads activated. Google Ads campaigns enabled. Real-time monitoring begins. Launch time: 6 AM Black Friday. Primary contact: Marketing Director.

nodes.label: Live Monitoring Dashboard
nodes.description: Real-time tracking of performance metrics across channels.
nodes.content: Dashboard setup in Google Data Studio. Metrics: Email open rate, click-through rate. Social: Impressions, engagement, CPC. Google Ads: CTR, conversions, ROAS. Alerts for: Spend exceeding 80% budget, CTR drop >20%, technical errors. Daily check-ins at 9 AM, 5 PM during campaign week.

nodes.label: Post-Campaign Analysis Report
nodes.description: Comprehensive report on performance, insights, ROI calculation.
nodes.content: Report sections: 1. Executive summary (ROAS, total revenue). 2. Channel breakdown (Email: $2.5k revenue, Social: $4k revenue, Google Ads: $5k revenue). 3. Creative performance (top-performing ad: Video #2). 4. Lessons learned (best day: Black Friday, best audience: retargeting). 5. Recommendations for next campaign. Deliverable: PDF report due 1 week post-campaign.

nodes.label: Campaign Retrospective & Handoff
nodes.description: Final review meeting, archive assets, hand off insights to marketing team.
nodes.content: Meeting agenda: Review report, discuss wins/failures, decide on asset reuse. Archive: All creatives stored in DAM. Data exported to CRM. Handoff: Insights documented in Confluence for future campaigns. Next steps: Plan Cyber Monday follow-up if ROAS >3.0.

request.prompt: We need to hire 5 engineers in the next 2 months. Our current process is just posting on LinkedIn and hoping. Build me a proper hiring pipeline.

response.message: Mission received. Building a structured hiring pipeline to secure 5 engineers in 2 months.

nodes.label: Hiring Mandate
nodes.description: Business requirement to hire 5 engineers within 2 months.
nodes.content: Objective: Hire 5 qualified engineers (roles: Backend, Frontend, Full-Stack) within 60 days. Budget approved. Current method (LinkedIn posts only) is insufficient.

nodes.label: Talent Sourcing Trigger
nodes.description: Initiates the multi-channel sourcing and outreach process.
nodes.content: Activate on receipt of hiring mandate. Launch coordinated sourcing across 5 channels: 1) LinkedIn Recruiter, 2) Employee referrals, 3) Niche job boards (e.g., Stack Overflow, AngelList), 4) Tech recruiting agency (contingency), 5) University/ bootcamp partnerships.

nodes.label: Candidate Pipeline State
nodes.description: Live tracker of candidates across all stages.
nodes.content: ATS (Greenhouse) dashboard. Metrics: Total applicants, sourced candidates, screening calls scheduled, technical interviews completed, offers extended. Target: Maintain a pipeline of 50+ qualified candidates in motion.

nodes.label: Structured Interview Process
nodes.description: Documented interview stages and scorecards.
nodes.content: Process: 1) Recruiter screen (30 min), 2) Technical phone screen (1 hr, live coding), 3) Take-home assignment (practical, 4-hr max), 4) On-site panel (3 hrs: system design, culture fit, peer review). Scorecards with clear rubrics for each stage.

nodes.label: Offer & Close Action
nodes.description: Execute offer generation, negotiation, and closing.
nodes.content: Upon hiring manager approval: 1) Generate competitive offer package (base, equity, benefits). 2) Conduct offer call with recruiter & hiring manager. 3) Manage negotiation within band. 4) Send formal offer letter and initiate background check.

nodes.label: Pipeline Review
nodes.description: Weekly review of pipeline health and velocity.
nodes.content: Weekly sync with Recruiter, Hiring Manager, and VP Engineering. Agenda: Review funnel metrics, identify bottlenecks (e.g., slow feedback), calibrate interview scores, adjust sourcing strategy. Goal: Maintain pace for 5 hires/60 days.

nodes.label: Hires Secured Output
nodes.description: Successful placement of 5 engineers.
nodes.content: Result: 5 signed offer letters and start dates confirmed. New engineers onboarded. Pipeline process documented and handed off for ongoing hiring.

request.prompt: Customers keep complaining their tickets go into a black hole. Design an escalation workflow — ticket comes in, auto-classify priority, route to right team, SLA tracking, escalate if overdue.

response.message: Ah, the classic 'black hole' complaint! A most vexing problem, mon ami. The tickets vanish, the customers fume, and the support teams are left in the dark. But fear not, for Hercule Poirot is on the case! We shall design a system of logic and order, a workflow that tracks, routes, and escalates with the precision of a Swiss watch. The little grey cells have been working. Voilà! I present the 'Ticket Vigilance & Escalation Protocol'.

nodes.label: New Ticket Submission
nodes.description: The initial customer request or issue is logged into the system.
nodes.content: Trigger: Ticket created via web form, email, or API. Payload includes: customer ID, subject, description, initial category (if provided), and timestamp.

nodes.label: Auto-Priority Classifier
nodes.description: AI/NLP model analyzes ticket content to assign a priority level (P0-Critical, P1-High, P2-Medium, P3-Low).
nodes.content: Action: Run classification model. Input: Ticket description, subject, customer tier. Output: Priority score and label. Rules: Keywords like 'down', 'outage', 'cannot work' trigger P0. Mentions of 'urgent' or high-value customer flag trigger P1.

nodes.label: Team Routing Engine
nodes.description: Routes the classified ticket to the correct support team based on topic and priority.
nodes.content: Action: Determine target queue. Logic: Technical bug -> Engineering. Billing question -> Finance. General how-to -> Customer Success. P0/P1 tickets always route to a dedicated 'Critical Response' team first.

nodes.label: SLA Tracking Clock
nodes.description: A stateful timer that monitors the ticket against its priority-based Service Level Agreement.
nodes.content: State: Tracks time since ticket assignment. SLA Targets: P0: 1 hour first response. P1: 4 hours. P2: 8 business hours. P3: 24 business hours. Continuously compares elapsed time vs. target.

nodes.label: Primary Support Triage
nodes.description: The assigned team's initial review and action on the ticket.
nodes.content: Action: Agent acknowledges, investigates, and provides initial response or solution. Expected outcome: Ticket status updated to 'In Progress' or 'Awaiting Customer Reply'.

nodes.label: Escalation Policy
nodes.description: The formal rules defining when and how a ticket must be escalated.
nodes.content: Policy Document: 1. If SLA for first response is breached by 15 minutes, escalate to team lead. 2. If ticket is unresolved after 2x the resolution SLA, escalate to senior management. 3. If customer replies twice without answer, auto-escalate one level. 4. All P0 breaches trigger immediate escalation to Head of Support.

nodes.label: Escalation Executor
nodes.description: The automated action that performs the escalation based on policy triggers.
nodes.content: Action: When triggered by the SLA Clock or policy rule: 1. Re-assign ticket to defined escalation queue (e.g., 'Team Lead', 'Management'). 2. Send high-priority alert via Slack/Email/PagerDuty. 3. Add internal note: 'ESCALATED: [Reason]'. 4. Notify original assignee.

nodes.label: Escalation Audit Log
nodes.description: An immutable record of all escalations, reasons, and outcomes for review.
nodes.content: Artifact: Log entry per escalation. Fields: Ticket ID, Timestamp, Escalation Reason (e.g., 'SLA Breach'), From (Agent/Team), To (Escalation Target), Policy Rule Triggered.

nodes.label: Resolved Ticket & Report
nodes.description: The final state of the ticket and a summary report for the cycle.
nodes.content: Output: 1. Ticket closed with resolution notes. 2. Customer satisfaction score (if collected). 3. Weekly report generated: Total tickets, % SLA met, # of escalations, root causes for breaches. This is the final deliverable.

request.prompt: Write a statement of work for a 3-month web application redesign project. Client is a mid-size e-commerce company. Budget is $85,000. Include scope, deliverables, timeline, and payment terms.

response.message:
# Statement of Work: E-Commerce Web Application Redesign

**Project Title:** E-Commerce Platform Redesign & Modernization
**Client:** [Client Name], a mid-size e-commerce company
**Vendor/Service Provider:** [Your Company/Agency Name]
**Effective Date:** [Date]
**Project Duration:** 3 Months (Approximately 12-13 weeks)
**Total Project Budget:** $85,000

---

## 1. Project Overview & Objectives

This Statement of Work (SOW) defines the terms, deliverables, and schedule for a comprehensive redesign of the Client's existing e-commerce web application. The primary objectives are to:
*   **Enhance User Experience (UX):** Create a more intuitive, engaging, and frictionless shopping journey to increase conversion rates and average order value.
*   **Modernize Visual Design (UI):** Implement a contemporary, brand-aligned visual identity that builds trust and improves aesthetic appeal.
*   **Improve Technical Performance:** Optimize application speed, mobile responsiveness, and core web vitals to boost SEO and user satisfaction.
*   **Strengthen Foundational Architecture:** Ensure the redesign establishes a maintainable, scalable codebase for future feature development.

## 2. Scope of Work

The Vendor will provide the following services and activities:

**2.1 Discovery & Strategy (Weeks 1-2)**
*   Stakeholder interviews to understand business goals, user personas, and key challenges.
*   Comprehensive audit of the existing website (analytics, user flows, SEO, content, and technical stack).
*   Competitive and market analysis.
*   Definition of key performance indicators (KPIs) and success metrics.

**2.2 User Experience (UX) Design (Weeks 3-5)**
*   Development of updated sitemap and information architecture.
*   Creation of user journey maps and user flow diagrams for key processes (e.g., product discovery, checkout).
*   Design of low-fidelity and high-fidelity interactive wireframes for all core templates, including:
    *   Homepage
    *   Category/Product Listing Pages (PLPs)
    *   Product Detail Pages (PDPs)
    *   Shopping Cart & Checkout Process (including guest checkout)
    *   User Account Dashboard
    *   Content Pages (About, Contact, FAQ)

**2.3 User Interface (UI) Design (Weeks 6-8)**
*   Development of a modern UI style guide including:
    *   Color palette, typography, iconography
    *   Button styles, form elements, and interactive component states
*   Creation of high-fidelity, pixel-perfect visual design mockups for all wireframed templates.
*   Design of responsive layouts for desktop, tablet, and mobile breakpoints.

**2.4 Front-End Development (Weeks 7-11)**
*   Translation of approved UI designs into clean, semantic HTML5, CSS3 (using a pre-processor like SASS), and JavaScript.
*   Implementation as a responsive, component-based front-end that can be integrated into the Client's chosen backend/CMS.
*   Focus on performance optimization (image compression, lazy loading, code minification).
*   Cross-browser and cross-device testing.
*   Basic ADA/WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for accessibility.

**2.5 Testing, Deployment & Transition (Weeks 11-12)**
*   User Acceptance Testing (UAT) support: providing a staging environment and facilitating bug tracking.
*   Coordination for deployment to the Client's production environment.
*   Provision of basic documentation (style guide, component documentation).
*   A 2-hour knowledge transfer session for the Client's team.

## 3. Out of Scope

The following items are explicitly **excluded** from this project budget and timeline:
*   Backend development, database changes, or API modifications.
*   Content migration from the old site to the new design (provided content must be final and delivered in agreed format).
*   Development of new e-commerce functionalities (e.g., wish lists, advanced filters, subscription models).
*   Third-party software or plugin licenses.
*   Ongoing hosting, maintenance, or support post-launch.
*   Extensive copywriting or professional photography.
*   Post-launch marketing or SEO campaigns.

## 4. Deliverables

All deliverables will be provided digitally via a shared project management platform (e.g., Figma, Zeplin, GitHub).

1.  **Strategy & Planning Document:** Includes audit findings, personas, and KPIs.
2.  **Interactive Wireframes:** For all core application templates.
3.  **UI Style Guide & Component Library:** Comprehensive design system documentation.
4.  **High-Fidelity Visual Design Mockups:** For all core templates at key breakpoints.
5.  **Fully Coded Front-End:** Production-ready HTML/CSS/JS files, organized and documented.
6.  **Testing Report & Bug Log:** From the UAT phase.
7.  **Final Project Documentation & Knowledge Transfer Session.**

## 5. Project Timeline & Milestones

*   **Kick-off & Project Start:** Week 1
*   **Milestone 1: Strategy & UX Approval:** End of Week 2
*   **Milestone 2: UI Design Approval:** End of Week 8
*   **Milestone 3: Front-End Development Complete & UAT Begins:** End of Week 11
*   **Milestone 4: UAT Sign-off & Final Deployment:** End of Week 12
*   **Project Closure & Knowledge Transfer:** Week 13

The project is scheduled for completion within **13 weeks** of the Effective Date. Any delays caused by the Client in providing feedback, content, or necessary approvals may adjust the final delivery date.

## 6. Client Responsibilities

To ensure project success, the Client agrees to:
*   Appoint a single primary decision-maker with authority to provide timely feedback and approvals.
*   Provide timely access to necessary stakeholders, analytics, brand assets, and existing platform logins.
*   Deliver all required written content, product data, and imagery in agreed formats by the dates specified in the project plan.
*   Provide timely, consolidated feedback at each review milestone (standard review cycle is 3 business days).
*   Provide a suitable staging/production environment and technical point of contact for deployment.

## 7. Payment Terms

The total fixed project fee is **$85,000**.

Payment will be structured across four milestones tied to deliverables and approvals:

*   **Payment 1 (25% - $21,250):** Due upon signing this SOW to initiate work.
*   **Payment 2 (25% - $21,250):** Due upon Client approval of Milestone 1 (Strategy & UX).
*   **Payment 3 (25% - $21,250):** Due upon Client approval of Milestone 2 (UI Design).
*   **Payment 4 (25% - $21,250):** Due upon final UAT sign-off and project completion.

All invoices are payable within **15 days** of receipt. Work may be paused if payments are overdue by more than 30 days.

## 8. Acceptance

This Statement of Work, along with the associated Master Services Agreement (if applicable), represents the entire agreement for this project.

**Approved By:**

________________________________________
[Client Representative Name & Title]
[Client Company Name]
Date: _______________

________________________________________
[Vendor Representative Name & Title]
[Vendor Company Name]
Date: _______________

request.prompt: What do we have so far?

response.message: Mission received. Current graph is empty. No nodes or edges exist. Ready for deployment.
